title: PATCH /api/user/password — update user password
description: Implement the endpoint to change the user's password. Requires the current password for verification.
acceptanceCriteria: PATCH /api/user/password with correct current_password and new_password returns 200
Wrong current_password returns 400 with message 'Current password is incorrect'
New password must be at least 8 characters — shorter returns 422
Password is stored as bcrypt hash after update
User can log in with the new password after update
Returns 401 if no valid JWT provided

steps.details: In src/api/schemas/user.py, add UpdatePasswordRequest(BaseModel) with: current_password (str), new_password (str, min_length=8).

In user router, add PATCH /password route. Handler: call verify_password(request.current_password, current_user.hashed_password), if False raise HTTPException(400, 'Current password is incorrect'), hash new password with hash_password(request.new_password), set current_user.hashed_password to new hash, commit. Return {"message": "Password updated successfully"}.

PATCH /api/user/password with correct current and new password — verify 200. Try logging in with new password — verify success. PATCH with wrong current password — verify 400. PATCH with new_password less than 8 chars — verify 422.

technicalNotes: Always verify current password before allowing password change
Never return the hashed password in the response
